    Sum = 0,
    for I <- 0..length(Nums)-1 do
        if bitshift(Mask, I) and 1 == 1 ->

        end
    end,

            length(Reachable) == length(S),
            lists:all(fun(Node) -> lists:member(Node, Reachable) end, S)

    Visited = [StartNode],
    Queue = [StartNode],
    
    do_bfs(Queue, Visited).

do_bfs([], Visited) ->
    Visited;
do_bfs([CurrentNode | QueueTail], Visited) ->
    % Find neighbors of CurrentNode that are also in S (induced subgraph)
    Neighbors = find_induced_neighbors(N, Edges, CurrentNode, Mask),
    
    NewNodes = lists:filter(fun(Neighbor) -> not lists:member(Neighbor, Visited) end, Neighbors),
    
    NewQueue = QueueTail ++ NewNodes,
    NewVisited = Visited ++ NewNodes,
    
    do_bfs(NewQueue, NewVisited).

%% Finds neighbors of a node that are present in the subset defined by Mask.
find_induced_neighbors(N, Edges, Node, Mask) ->
    Neighbors = [],
    % Iterate through all edges to find those connected to Node
    for [U, V] <- Edges do
        if U == Node ->
            Neighbor = V,

-module(solver).
-export([solve/2]).

%% Helper function for BFS/DFS connectivity check
-spec is_connected(integer(), list(integer()), list(integer()), list(integer())) -> boolean().
is_connected(start_node, nodes_in_subset, adj, all_nodes) ->
    case nodes_in_subset of
        [] -> true; % Should not happen for non-empty subset
        [s] -> true; % Single node is always connected
        _ ->
            % BFS starting from start_node
            bfs(start_node, [start_node], {start_node})
    end.

bfs(current_node, visited, {unvisited_nodes}) ->
    % This implementation relies on the structure of the recursive call to track connectivity.
    % We use a set/map conceptually, but in Erlang, lists are common for small N.
    true. % Placeholder, full BFS logic is complex in pure recursive Erlang without Maps/Sets readily available for efficiency.

bfs(start_node, visited, _all_nodes) ->
    % A simplified connectivity check for this problem structure (N<=13)
    % We iterate over all nodes in the subset and check reachability from the first node.
    case start_node of
        _ ->
            % Perform BFS/DFS to see if all nodes in nodes_in_subset are reachable from start_node
            Reachable <- perform_bfs(start_node, nodes_in_subset, adj)
            is_subset(nodes_in_subset, Reachable)
    end.

perform_bfs(start, subset, adj) ->
    % subset: the set of nodes we care about
    % adj: the adjacency list representation of the induced subgraph
    bfs_util(start, [start], [start], subset, adj).

bfs_util(queue, visited, reachable, subset, adj) ->
    case queue of
        [] -> reachable;
        [u | rest_queue] ->
            % Find neighbors of u that are in the subset and not yet visited
            Neighbors = lists:filter(fun(v) -> lists:member(v, subset) and not lists:member(v, visited) end, adj[u]).

            New_Visited = visited ++ Neighbors.
            New_Reachable = reachable ++ Neighbors.
            New_Queue = rest_queue ++ Neighbors.

            bfs_util(New_Queue, New_Visited, New_Reachable, subset, adj).
    end.

is_subset(subset, superset) ->
    lists:all(fun(x) -> lists:member(x, superset) end, subset).

%% Main solver function
-spec solve([integer()], [[integer()]]) -> integer().
solve(nums, edges) ->
    N = length(nums),
    Adj = build_adjacency_list(N, edges),

    TotalCount = 0,
    % Iterate over all non-empty subsets represented by bitmasks from 1 to 2^N - 1
    % 2^N is 1 `shift_left` N
    MaxMask = 1 `shift_left` N - 1,

    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Mask) ->
        if Mask == 0 -> Acc;
        true ->
            Subset = bits_to_nodes(N, Mask),
            Sum = calculate_sum(nums, Subset),
            Connected = check_connectivity(Subset, Adj),

            if Connected and (mod(Sum, 2) == 0) ->
                Acc + 1;
            true ->
                Acc
            end
        end,
        0,
        lists:seq(1, MaxMask)
    end, Adj).

% --- Utility Functions ---

%% Build adjacency list from edges
build_adjacency_list(N, Edges) ->
    % Initialize Adj as a list of lists, size N, all empty
    EmptyAdj = lists:replicate(N, []),
    
    % Fold over edges to populate the adjacency list
    lists:foldl(fun(Adj, [U, V]) ->
        Adj1 = lists:update(Adj, U, [V | Adj[U]]),
        Adj2 = lists:update(Adj1, V, [U | Adj1[V]]),
        Adj2
    end,
    EmptyAdj,
    Edges).

%% Convert bitmask to list of node indices
bits_to_nodes(N, Mask) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, I) ->
        if Mask and (1 `shift_left` I) ->
            [I | Acc];
        true ->
            Acc
        end,
        [],
        lists:seq(0, N - 1)
    end, Mask).

%% Calculate sum of values for nodes in subset
calculate_sum(nums, Subset) ->
    lists:sum([nums[i] || i <- Subset]).

%% Check connectivity of the induced subgraph for a subset
check_connectivity(Subset, Adj) ->

        [] -> false; % Should not happen based on loop structure
        [S] -> true; % Single node is always connected
        _ ->
            StartNode = hd(Subset),
            % Perform BFS/DFS restricted to nodes in Subset
            Reachable <- perform_bfs(StartNode, Subset, Adj),
            % Check if all nodes in Subset are reachable
            lists:length(Subset) == lists:length(Reachable)
    end.

%% BFS implementation for connectivity check
perform_bfs(start_node, subset, adj) ->
    % queue, visited, reachable
    bfs_util([start_node], [start_node], [start_node], subset, adj).

bfs_util(Queue, Visited, Reachable, subset, adj) ->
    case Queue of
        [] -> Reachable;
        [U | RestQueue] ->
            % Find neighbors of U that are in the subset, not in Visited
            Neighbors = lists:filter(fun(V) -> 
                lists:member(V, subset) and 
                not lists:member(V, Visited)
            end, adj[U]).

            NewVisited = Visited ++ Neighbors.
            NewReachable = Reachable ++ Neighbors.
            NewQueue = RestQueue ++ Neighbors.

            bfs_util(NewQueue, NewVisited, NewReachable, subset, adj)
    end.

%% Simple helper for list update (equivalent to array update in imperative style)
lists:update(List, Index, NewValue) ->
    case lists:nth(Index, List) of
        _ when Index >= 0 and Index < length(List) ->
            lists:replace(List, Index, NewValue);
        _ ->
            List
    end.
